title: euphrasia salisburgensis subsp. cuprea
intro: Euphrasia salisburgensis subsp. cuprea, also known as the Salzburg eye-clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe and protect the eyes, skin, and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Euphrasia salisburgensis subsp. cuprea include its ability to reduce eye fatigue, improve vision, and alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis and other eye conditions, as well as its potential to help with wound healing and skin conditions. Euphrasia salisburgensis subsp. cuprea can be prepared as a tincture, eye drops, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote eye and skin health.
